frameworks.name: counterfactual reasoning
frameworks.one_line: responsibility depends on whether alternative behavior could have altered the outcome

frameworks.name: causal attribution
frameworks.one_line: produced effort or force as direct cause of outcome drives responsibility

frameworks.name: dual-factor responsibility model
frameworks.one_line: combines produced and counterfactual effort into a single attribution account

core_question: How do people attribute responsibility to individuals in collaborative tasks?
key_assumptions: responsibility judgments can be modeled from effort-related metrics
candidate metrics include produced effort, competence, force, and counterfactual effort
collaborative-task judgments are decomposable across individual contributors
manipulations: counterfactual effort (whether alternative behavior could have altered outcome)
key_findings: participant judgments are best described by a model that uses both produced and counterfactual effort
models using only produced effort/force or only counterfactuals fit worse
the dual-factor pattern generalized to an independent validation dataset
interpretations: responsibility attribution in collaborative tasks reflects a dual-factor account combining produced and counterfactual effort
